result.stdout:
The external FluentMigrator runner that integrates into the .NET Core CLI tooling

Usage: dotnet-fm [options] [command]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help  Execute FluentMigrator actions

Commands:
  list          List stuff
  migrate       Apply migrations
  rollback      Rollback last migration
  validate      Validations

Run 'dotnet-fm [command] --help' for more information about a command.

List stuff

Usage: dotnet-fm list [options] [command]

Options:

Commands:
  migrations    List migrations

Run 'list [command] --help' for more information about a command.

commands.payload:
List stuff

Usage: dotnet-fm list [options] [command]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help  Show help information

Commands:
  migrations    List migrations
  processors    List processors

Run 'list [command] --help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
List migrations

Usage: dotnet-fm list migrations [options]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help                                  Show help information
  -c|--connection <CONNECTION_STRING_OR_NAME>   The name of the connection string (falls back to machine name) or the connection string itself to the server and database you want to execute your migrations against.
  --no-connection                               Indicates that migrations will be generated without consulting a target database. Should only be used when generating an output file.
  -p|--processor <PROCESSOR_NAME>               The kind of database you are migrating against. Available choices can be shown with "list processors".
  -s|--processor-switches <PROCESSOR_SWITCHES>  Provider specific switches.
  --preview                                     Only output the SQL generated by the migration - do not execute it. Default is false.
  -V|--verbose                                  Show the SQL statements generated and execution time in the console. Default is false.
  --profile <PROFILE>                           The profile to run after executing migrations.
  --context <CONTEXT>                           Set ApplicationContext to the given string.
  --timeout <TIMEOUT_SEC>                       Overrides the default database command timeout of 30 seconds.
  -o|--output[:<FILENAME>]                      Output generated SQL to a file. Default is no output. A filename may be specified, otherwise [targetAssemblyName].sql is the default.
  -a|--assembly <ASSEMBLY_NAME>                 The assemblies containing the migrations you want to execute.
  -n|--namespace <NAMESPACE>                    The namespace contains the migrations you want to run. Default is all migrations found within the Target Assembly will be run.
  --nested                                      Whether migrations in nested namespaces should be included. Used in conjunction with the namespace option.
  --start-version                               The specific version to start migrating from. Only used when NoConnection is true. Default is 0.
  --working-directory <WORKING_DIRECTORY>       The directory to load SQL scripts specified by migrations from.
  -t|--tag                                      Filters the migrations to be run by tag.
  -b|--allow-breaking-changes                   Allows execution of migrations marked as breaking changes.
  --default-schema-name                         Set default schema name for VersionInfo table and the migrations.
  --strip                                       Strip comments from the SQL scripts. Default is true.
  --include-untagged-migrations                 Include untagged migrations.
  --include-untagged-maintenances               Include untagged maintenances.

result.stdout:
Apply migrations

Usage: dotnet-fm migrate [options] [command]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help                                  Show help information
  -m|--transaction-mode <MODE>                  Overrides the transaction behavior of migrations, so that all migrations to be executed will run in one transaction.
  -c|--connection <CONNECTION_STRING_OR_NAME>   The name of the connection string (falls back to machine name) or the connection string itself to the server and database you want to execute your migrations against.
  --no-connection                               Indicates that migrations will be generated without consulting a target database. Should only be used when generating an output file.
  -p|--processor <PROCESSOR_NAME>               The kind of database you are migrating against. Available choices can be shown with "list processors".
  -s|--processor-switches <PROCESSOR_SWITCHES>  Provider specific switches.
  --preview                                     Only output the SQL generated by the migration - do not execute it. Default is false.
  -V|--verbose                                  Show the SQL statements generated and execution time in the console. Default is false.
  --profile <PROFILE>                           The profile to run after executing migrations.
  --context <CONTEXT>                           Set ApplicationContext to the given string.
  --timeout <TIMEOUT_SEC>                       Overrides the default database command timeout of 30 seconds.
  -o|--output[:<FILENAME>]                      Output generated SQL to a file. Default is no output. A filename may be specified, otherwise [targetAssemblyName].sql is the default.
  -a|--assembly <ASSEMBLY_NAME>                 The assemblies containing the migrations you want to execute.
  -n|--namespace <NAMESPACE>                    The namespace contains the migrations you want to run. Default is all migrations found within the Target Assembly will be run.
  --nested                                      Whether migrations in nested namespaces should be included. Used in conjunction with the namespace option.
  --start-version                               The specific version to start migrating from. Only used when NoConnection is true. Default is 0.
  --working-directory <WORKING_DIRECTORY>       The directory to load SQL scripts specified by migrations from.
  -t|--tag                                      Filters the migrations to be run by tag.
  -b|--allow-breaking-changes                   Allows execution of migrations marked as breaking changes.
  --default-schema-name                         Set default schema name for VersionInfo table and the migrations.
  --strip                                       Strip comments from the SQL scripts. Default is true.
  --include-untagged-migrations                 Include untagged migrations.
  --include-untagged-maintenances               Include untagged maintenances.

Commands:
  down                                          Revert migrations
  up                                            Apply migrations

Run 'migrate [command] --help' for more information about a command.

Rollback last migration

Usage: dotnet-fm rollback [options] [command]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help                                  Show help information
  -m|--transaction-mode <MODE>                  Overrides the transaction behavior of migrations, so that all migrations to be executed will run in one transaction.
  -c|--connection <CONNECTION_STRING_OR_NAME>   The name of the connection string (falls back to machine name) or the connection string itself to the server and database you want to execute your migrations against.
  --no-connection                               Indicates that migrations will be generated without consulting a target database. Should only be used when generating an output file.
  -p|--processor <PROCESSOR_NAME>               The kind of database you are migrating against. Available choices can be shown with "list processors".
  -s|--processor-switches <PROCESSOR_SWITCHES>  Provider specific switches.
  --preview                                     Only output the SQL generated by the migration - do not execute it. Default is false.
  -V|--verbose                                  Show the SQL statements generated and execution time in the console. Default is false.
  --profile <PROFILE>                           The profile to run after executing migrations.
  --context <CONTEXT>                           Set ApplicationContext to the given string.
  --timeout <TIMEOUT_SEC>                       Overrides the default database command timeout of 30 seconds.
  -o|--output[:<FILENAME>]                      Output generated SQL to a file. Default is no output. A filename may be specified, otherwise [targetAssemblyName].sql is the default.
  -a|--assembly <ASSEMBLY_NAME>                 The assemblies containing the migrations you want to execute.
  -n|--namespace <NAMESPACE>                    The namespace contains the migrations you want to run. Default is all migrations found within the Target Assembly will be run.
  --nested                                      Whether migrations in nested namespaces should be included. Used in conjunction with the namespace option.
  --start-version                               The specific version to start migrating from. Only used when NoConnection is true. Default is 0.
  --working-directory <WORKING_DIRECTORY>       The directory to load SQL scripts specified by migrations from.
  -t|--tag                                      Filters the migrations to be run by tag.
  -b|--allow-breaking-changes                   Allows execution of migrations marked as breaking changes.
  --default-schema-name                         Set default schema name for VersionInfo table and the migrations.
  --strip                                       Strip comments from the SQL scripts. Default is true.
  --include-untagged-migrations                 Include untagged migrations.
  --include-untagged-maintenances               Include untagged maintenances.

Commands:
  all                                           Rollback all migrations
  by                                            Rollback migrations
  to                                            Rollback migrations up to a given version

Run 'rollback [command] --help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Validate the order of applied migrations

Usage: dotnet-fm validate versions [options]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help                                  Show help information
  -c|--connection <CONNECTION_STRING_OR_NAME>   The name of the connection string (falls back to machine name) or the connection string itself to the server and database you want to execute your migrations against.

  -s|--processor-switches <PROCESSOR_SWITCHES>  Provider specific switches.

  --working-directory <WORKING_DIRECTORY>       The directory to load SQL scripts specified by migrations from.
  -t|--tag                                      Filters the migrations to be run by tag.
  -b|--allow-breaking-changes                   Allows execution of migrations marked as breaking changes.

  --include-untagged-migrations                 Include untagged migrations.

The assemblies containing the migrations you want to execute.
The name of the connection string (falls back to machine name) or the connection string itself to the server and database you want to execute your migrations against.
Set ApplicationContext to the given string.
Set default schema name for VersionInfo table and the migrations.
Include untagged maintenances.
The namespace contains the migrations you want to run. Default is all migrations found within the Target Assembly will be run.
Whether migrations in nested namespaces should be included. Used in conjunction with the namespace option.
Indicates that migrations will be generated without consulting a target database. Should only be used when generating an output file.
Output generated SQL to a file. Default is no output. A filename may be specified, otherwise [targetAssemblyName].sql is the default.
Only output the SQL generated by the migration - do not execute it. Default is false.
The kind of database you are migrating against. Available choices can be shown with "list processors".
Provider specific switches.
The profile to run after executing migrations.
The specific version to start migrating from. Only used when NoConnection is true. Default is 0.
Filters the migrations to be run by tag.
Overrides the default database command timeout of 30 seconds.
Show the SQL statements generated and execution time in the console. Default is false.
The directory to load SQL scripts specified by migrations from.
